heading: Common chart pitfalls
intro: These examples show common issues and better implementations. Use them to spot risks early and improve consistency.

examples.title: Example 1: Truncated axis and unclear labels

panels.label: Don't
panels.description: Column chart starts at 90, labels are rotated and the title does not explain the timeframe.
panels.ariaLabel: Column chart with truncated axis and rotated labels.
bullets: Exaggerates differences by truncating the axis.
Missing units and time period.
Long labels are hard to read.

panels.label: Do
panels.description: Use a horizontal bar chart that starts at zero with direct labels and a clear timeframe.
panels.ariaLabel: Horizontal bar chart starting at zero with clear labels.

table.caption: Service completions by region (Jan to Jun 2025)

bullets: Set the chart canvas to a white background.
Start the axis at zero or clearly indicate truncation.
Include timeframe and unit in the title.
Keep labels readable and aligned.

panels.label: Don't
panels.description: Stacked bar uses red and green only, with no labels and low contrast.
panels.ariaLabel: Stacked bar chart using red and green only with no labels.
bullets: Relies on colour alone to communicate meaning.
Insufficient contrast for some users.
No direct labels or markers.

panels.label: Do
panels.description: Use accessible colours, clear contrast, and a supporting table for labels.
panels.ariaLabel: Stacked bar chart with accessible colours and clear labels.
bullets: Set the chart canvas to a white background.
Provide labels in a table, not colour alone.
Meet WCAG contrast requirements.
Keep the chart clear in greyscale and colour-blind testing.

panels.label: Don't
panels.description: Line chart shows values only on hover with no table or summary.
panels.ariaLabel: Line chart with values available only on hover.
bullets: Keyboard and touch users cannot access key values.
No text alternative for screen readers.
Users cannot verify exact values.

panels.label: Do
panels.description: Include a summary and a semantic table for the same data.
panels.ariaLabel: Line chart with a summary and data table.
panels.summary: Applications rose steadily from January to May, with a small drop in June.
bullets: Set the chart canvas to a white background.
Use summary text for the key insight.
Provide precise values in a table.
Keep interactions keyboard accessible.

panels.label: Don't
panels.description: Using many small slices makes comparison difficult and creates visual clutter.
panels.ariaLabel: Pie chart with twelve segments that are difficult to compare.
bullets: Too many slices reduce readability.
Small segments are hard to compare accurately.
Large legends force users to cross-reference excessively.

panels.label: Do
panels.description: Keep pie segments limited so users can quickly compare relative share.
panels.ariaLabel: Pie chart with four clearly distinguishable segments and direct labels.
bullets: Set the chart canvas to a white background.
Limit pie segments to 2 to 4 where possible.
Use labels and pattern support for clear distinction.
Include direct labels, a clear title and percentages.
